meaning: n.[希神]卡珊德拉,凶事预言家,不为人所信的预言家
examples: Cassandra noticed him because he was good-looking.
"Why don't you borrow your sister's car?" said Cassandra stiffly.
The Trojans should have listened to Cassandra.
There have been two approaches: Riak and Cassandra stress availability, while HBase has stressed consistency.
Jane Austen's sister, Cassandra, destroyed all the letters she and Jane had exchanged.
